Georgia

Getting Your Occupational Therapist License in Georgia

Here's what you need to know.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

Licensing Path

Georgia has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not issuing yet. For now, out-of-state applicants still use endorsement.

Endorsement fee
$0
Processing time
4 to 6 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Available
Compact
OT Compact enacted, not active yet
Board
Georgia Board of Occupational Therapy

From the board

Endorsement Steps

  1. Complete Education & Fieldwork

    Earn a master's or doctoral degree in occupational therapy from an ACOTE-accredited program and complete specific fieldwork requirements.

  2. Pass the NBCOT Examination

    Pass the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) exam for OTRs.

  3. Submit Application and Fees

    Complete the state application, undergo a background check if required, and submit the necessary licensing fees.

Quick Facts

Total fee
Processing time
4 to 6 Weeks
Renewal fee
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years

Fees

Application
N/A
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost

License Types

Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR): The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Georgia.

Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA): Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

Required Documents

  • Official Transcripts: Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • NBCOT Score Report: Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

Georgia Board of Occupational Therapy

Source: Primary board website

Renewal

Renewal fee is . Renewal cycle: Every 2 years.

Georgia Occupational Therapist Licensing FAQ

How much does a Georgia OT license cost?

The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

Is NBCOT certification required?

Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

Does Georgia have reciprocity for occupational therapists?

Georgia has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Georgia?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

Sources

Georgia Board of Occupational Therapy

OT Compact

This page is maintained as a reference for occupational therapists and recruiters evaluating Georgia assignments.
